fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oklahoma Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oklahoma - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity oklahoma, how to transfer physical therapist license to oklahoma, physical therapist compact oklahoma, oklahoma physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure — PT
board.parent_dept: Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision
board.mailing_address: Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision, 101 NE 51st Street, Oklahoma City, OK 73105-1821

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Oklahoma.

steps.title: 1. Create an Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the Board's Online Portal System (okmb.online/) and complete the appropriate application for initial licensure (by examination or endorsement), paying the required application fee.

steps.title: 2. Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying completion of your CAPTE-accredited physical therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision.

steps.title: 3. Complete Background Check and Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check through an approved vendor. Additionally, you must pass the Oklahoma Jurisprudence Examination, which is accessible through the Board's Online Portal System.

steps.title: 4. Provide NPTE Scores or License Verifications
steps.description: If applying by examination, have your official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores sent directly to the Board by the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT). If applying by endorsement, arrange for official verification of all current and prior PT licenses to be sent directly from other state boards to Oklahoma, preferably through a service like VeriDoc.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Oklahoma physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $200. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Oklahoma participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Oklahoma without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Oklahoma?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Oklahoma
hero.dek: Physical Therapists applying for licensure in Oklahoma must use the Board's Online Portal System to submit their application.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Board's Online Portal System, click 'Register' to create a new user account, and follow the prompts to set up your login credentials. Once registered and logged in, you can select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Initial PT License by Examination' or 'Initial PT License by Endorsement') to begin.
